fix: shorter IDs, startup API fetch, and tokens guard (#45)

* fix: shorter IDs, startup API fetch, and tokens guard

Three fixes for cross-device sync usability:

1. IDs: Changed from long base-36 strings to 8-char uppercase
   alphanumeric codes (e.g., '7K3X4MBN') using an unambiguous
   charset (no 0/O/1/I). Uses timestamp+counter+random for
   collision resistance.

2. Startup sync: SessionContext now fetches all sessions from
   the API on mount and merges them with localStorage state
   (higher version wins). New MERGE_REMOTE_SESSIONS reducer
   action. GameViewPage falls back to API fetch when game is
   not in localStorage.

3. Tokens guard: NightFlashcard uses nullish coalescing on
   playerSeat.tokens to prevent TypeError for older game data.

Streaming render migration and buffer elimination

- Convert all render functions to write directly to out: TextIO
  instead of returning strings; use sep='' forward-pass pattern
  to handle separators without lookahead or wrappers.

- Eliminate lines lists in diagram body builders and tree renderers:
  _render_markdown_node, _render_html_node, _write_ltac_node_normalized,
  render_info all write directly to out using a first=[True] mutable flag.
  _sacm_collect_edges and _gsn_collect_edges now take a write_edge
  callback instead of an edge_lines list. _sacm_diagram_body uses two
  DFS passes (collect dot decls, then write edges); _gsn_diagram_body
  writes BottomPadding after edges (cosmetic ordering change in Mermaid
  source, golden files updated).

- Replace io.StringIO whole-document buffer in _inline_rewrite_file with
  direct streaming to a temp file. Line-ending detection reads only the
  first 4 KB. No content comparison after writing (mtime may change).

- Replace two-pass --fixmissing algorithm with a single streaming pass:
  _collect_document_element_ids pre-scans existing element IDs; smart
  placement (_emit_stubs_after, _emit_all_remaining) inserts missing
  stubs inline relative to their LTAC predecessors without buffering.
  Removes _scan_region_ends, _find_epilogue_line, _insert_missing_stubs.

- Add docs/streaming.md documenting the architecture, separator pattern,
  and all changed function signatures.

- Update AGENTS.md with streaming API conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix stray vertices causing 912m bounding box and Vision Pro crash

Root cause: Array, Solidify, and Bevel modifiers on Blender objects were not
applied before export. The USD exporter evaluated these modifiers during export,
creating geometry spanning up to 912 meters (vs actual model ~21m). This caused
GPU depth buffer issues on real Vision Pro hardware, triggering device reset.

Fix: Apply all modifiers before per-material join in export script.
Re-exported all 18 USDZ parts with modifiers applied.
Collection 7 re-exported unmerged (205 meshes, 5.8MB vs 702MB merged).

Verified: max coordinate in all parts now ≤ 21 (was 910, 430, 203, 117).
Estimated draw calls: ~315 (down from 364).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix NRE in AddUniversalDynamicSymbol for invalid symbol address ranges (#2376)

When a ProcessSymbol event has an invalid address range (e.g.,
StartAddress=0x0, EndAddress=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF from zeroed
/proc/kallsyms on Linux without root), the ETLX conversion crashed
with a NullReferenceException.

Three fixes applied:
1. Skip symbols where EndAddress <= StartAddress (invalid range)
2. Change (int) to (long) cast for the size parameter to match
   ForAllUnresolvedCodeAddressesInRange's parameter type
3. Add null guard on loadedModule as defense-in-depth

Fixes #2373

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update Code Security SCA Page (#35113)

* Update Code Security SCA Page

This PR updates the Software Composition Analysis (SCA) documentation to make the product model and UI views easier to understand.

Changes:
1. Adds a How it works section that explains Static SCA (repo scans) vs Runtime SCA (APM) and how advisory ingestion impacts results.
2. Reorganizes content into Key capabilities with clearer user workflows (prioritize vulnerabilities, view by repository, PR Gates, library inventory, APM context).
3. Adds Understanding SCA views to explain the difference between Repositories Explorer (commit-scoped snapshot) and Vulnerabilities Explorer (continuously matched), including an example.
4. Documents retroactive advisory matching so customers understand why new CVEs can appear without rerunning scans.
5. Keeps existing setup and language support references and updates “Next steps” accordingly.
